status.title: ActionTriggeredStatus
status.description:
Delivery status of a single action after a decision fires.

triggered    — action was invoked and delivery succeeded
skipped      — fire_on rule not met (e.g. fire_on='true' but decision=False)
failed       — action was attempted but delivery raised an error
would_trigger — dry_run mode; action would have fired but was not invoked

type.title: ErrorType
type.description:
Canonical error type identifiers. The string value is the wire value.

Rules (from API spec):
  - Callers must branch on .type, never on .message.
  - Message text is for humans; type is for machines.
  - The enum spelling is the ground truth for YAML, HTTP, and Python alike.
    In particular: z_score (not zscore), not_found (not 404), conflict (not 409).

error.title: ErrorDetail
error.description:
The inner ``error`` object serialised inside ErrorResponse.

This is the exact shape the API spec requires:
  { "type": "...", "message": "...", "location": null, "suggestion": null }

Callers must branch on ``type``, never on ``message``.

anyOf.title: ErrorResponse

schema.title: ActionTriggered
schema.description:
Delivery record for a single action invocation after a condition fires.

Actions are best-effort — status='failed' does NOT roll back the decision.
The full pipeline response returns HTTP 200 regardless of action outcomes.

payload_sent is populated for triggered actions (webhook / workflow payloads).
error is populated only when status='failed'.
